title: anthyllis gracilis
intro: Anthyllis gracilis, also known as Kidney Vetch or Buckbean, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Anthyllis gracilis include its potential to treat kidney and urinary tract issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support kidney health and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections.
